title: Contact form cannot deliver messages: action="#" on a static host with no submit handler
reproduction_steps: Open /contacto
Fill Nombre/Correo/Mensaje
Press Enviar
POST goes to action="#" (same static URL); no backend or JS processes it; the message is never delivered
expected_behavior: Submitting the form delivers the message to the community or clearly routes the user to a working channel
actual_behavior: Form renders and validates client-side but submission has no processing endpoint; data is silently lost
root_cause: Static maqueta shipped with a placeholder action="#" and no handler; Hostinger static docroot serves the same page back on POST
user_impact: Visitors who choose the form believe they contacted the community; nobody receives the message. Primary conversion journey fails silently
business_impact: Lost inquiries and participation requests; erodes trust
recommendation: Short term: remove the non-functional form and present the already-working WhatsApp/email channels as the primary contact CTA. Long term (product decision): wire the form to a real processor (Hostinger PHP mail handler or a form service) with spam protection
desired_outcome: No visitor-facing contact path that silently discards input
acceptance_criteria: /contacto contains no form whose submission is undeliverable
Contact via visible channels (WhatsApp, mailto) remains fully functional
If a form returns, an end-to-end delivery test in a test inbox passes
resolution_steps: Replace the <form> block with a contact CTA section reusing existing WhatsApp/mailto links
Keep labels/icons consistent with design system
Later, separately: implement real form processing if the community decides to have one
minimal_safe_fix: Remove the dead form; promote WhatsApp/email CTAs (contacto/index.html already contains both at line 149)
durable_fix: PHP mail endpoint or external form service with CSRF/honeypot + success/error states; requires product decision and testing
proposed_changes: Delete form element lines 127-147, replace with .contact-cta block linking wa.me/573206627608 and mailto:caminodeldharma1@gmail.com
example_fix: Escríbenos por WhatsApp Escríbenos un correo
validation_procedure: Load /contacto: no <form> with dead action remains
Keyboard-navigate the new CTAs
Verify WhatsApp/mailto links open correct targets
validation_commands: curl -s https://caminodeldharma.org/contacto | grep -c '<form'  # expect 0 after deploy of minimal fix
regression_tests: Contact journey manual test on mobile and desktop
monitoring_signals: GA4 events on contact CTA clicks (optional)
tradeoffs: Losing an on-page form may reduce contact for users unwilling to use WhatsApp/email; mitigated by durable fix decision
human_decisions_required: Whether the community wants a real form backend (durable fix)

title: Calendar .ics download 404s: relative path breaks under the slashless canonical URL policy
reproduction_steps: Open /eventos/encuentro-nacional-2026
Click 'Añadir al calendario'
Click 'Apple Calendar' or 'Descargar archivo .ics'
Request to https://caminodeldharma.org/ical/encuentro-nacional-2026.ics returns 404
expected_behavior: Both options download a valid VCALENDAR file matching the event data
actual_behavior: On /eventos and /eventos/encuentro-nacional-2026 the .ics option 404s. Browser-verified resolution: attributes are RELATIVE ('ical/...' and '../ical/...'); under slashless canonical URLs the last segment is treated as a file, so both resolve to /ical/encuentro-nacional-2026.ics -> 404. The real file exists at /eventos/ical/encuentro-nacional-2026.ics and returns 200 (served as text/plain, not text/calendar). Only ONE event offers calendar options; 'Pausa Profunda Cali' is EventCompleted and correctly offers none.
root_cause: Relative data-calendar-ics paths combined with the site's slashless canonical URL policy (301 strips trailing slash), which changes the relative-resolution base to the site root. Secondary: .ics MIME served as text/plain.
user_impact: Two of the four 'Add to calendar' options (.ics download, Apple Calendar) fail on the single upcoming event; Google and Outlook options work
business_impact: Minor friction on event signup; smaller than originally recorded (one event, not two)
recommendation: Replace the relative paths with absolute ones: /eventos/ical/encuentro-nacional-2026.ics in both eventos/index.html and eventos/encuentro-nacional-2026/index.html; add AddType text/calendar .ics to .htaccess. Do NOT create new .ics files — the file already exists (the originally prescribed fix would have duplicated it).
desired_outcome: All four calendar options work for both events
acceptance_criteria: On /eventos and /eventos/encuentro-nacional-2026 the resolved .ics URL returns 200
content-type is text/calendar
Google/Outlook options unchanged
Completed events still show no calendar options
resolution_steps: Author both .ics files from the JSON-LD event data
Add MIME mapping if needed (AddType text/calendar .ics in .htaccess) — verify server default first
Deploy; verify status, content type and import
minimal_safe_fix: Absolute path in the two data-calendar-ics attributes
durable_fix: Absolute paths + text/calendar MIME + a release check that any new event ships its .ics with an absolute reference
example_fix:
BEGIN:VCALENDAR
VERSION:2.0
PRODID:-//Camino del Dharma//Eventos//ES
BEGIN:VEVENT
UID:encuentro-nacional-2026@caminodeldharma.org
DTSTART;VALUE=DATE:20260807
DTEND;VALUE=DATE:20260810
SUMMARY:7.º Encuentro Nacional Buddhista – 2026
LOCATION:Casa Retiro San Pablo\, Puerto Colombia
URL:https://caminodeldharma.org/eventos/encuentro-nacional-2026
END:VEVENT
END:VCALENDAR
validation_procedure: Download both files from production
Import into at least one calendar client
Cross-check fields against JSON-LD
regression_tests: Calendar dialog still opens and Google/Outlook options unchanged
monitoring_signals: 404 logs for /ical/ paths drop to zero
rollback_procedure: Remove the files (returns to prior 404 state) or fix content
tradeoffs: Absolute paths couple markup to the /eventos/ical/ location; acceptable given stable URL policy (ADR 0008)
residual_risks: Past event (pausa-profunda-cali) file is low value but link is still exposed on its page

title: HSTS not yet active on caminodeldharma.org (candidate header commented in .htaccess)
reproduction_steps: curl -sS -D - https://caminodeldharma.org/ | grep -i strict-transport-security → no output
expected_behavior: HTTPS responses carry Strict-Transport-Security so browsers refuse downgraded connections after first visit
actual_behavior: No HSTS header on any response class; first-visit HTTP request remains interceptable before the 301
root_cause: Header intentionally commented pending this audit (line 103 of .htaccess)
user_impact: SSL-stripping window on first/expired visits over hostile networks
business_impact: Small but real transport-integrity gap; blocks security-header maturity
recommendation: APLAZADA 2026-07-20 por decisión del propietario (ADR 0020): no activar HSTS mientras dure la transición a WordPress. El análisis técnico sigue vigente — no hay bloqueadores y la cabecera solo-host es segura — pero el corte puede alterar hosting, TLS y redirects, y con ~9 clics en 28 días el riesgo que HSTS mitiga (downgrade en la primera visita) es menor que el riesgo operativo de fijar política durante la migración. Revisar tras el corte + ≥30 días estables, decidiendo entonces entre entrada escalonada o valor duradero directo. includeSubDomains y preload siguen RECHAZADOS.
desired_outcome: HSTS max-age=604800 (Phase 1) served on all intended HTTPS responses of the primary host; max-age=31536000 deferred to Phase 2 post-WordPress (ADR 0018)
acceptance_criteria: STS header exactly max-age=604800 present on HTTPS 200/301/404 responses of caminodeldharma.org and www (Phase 1)
No includeSubDomains, no preload, no one-year max-age during the transition
Header not relied upon over plain HTTP (browsers ignore it there per RFC 6797)
minimal_safe_fix: Activate the Phase-1 line exactly: Header always set Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=604800" — nothing else
durable_fix: Phase 2 (ADR 0018): raise to max-age=31536000 after WordPress is stable in production >=30 days; later, separately: evaluate includeSubDomains after subdomain inventory (TASK-0012)
proposed_changes:
- # Header always set Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=604800"
+ Header always set Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=604800"
validation_procedure: curl HTTPS apex/www on 200, 301, 404 → header present with exact Phase-1 value max-age=604800
curl HTTP apex → 301 unchanged
Browse site: no breakage
regression_tests: Full navigation smoke test over HTTPS after deploy
monitoring_signals: Certificate expiry monitoring (cert valid to 2026-10-12; renewal must not lapse once HSTS pins clients)
Reminder to execute Phase 2 (max-age=31536000) after stable WordPress cutover (ADR 0018)
rollback_triggers: Unexpected breakage attributable to the header
rollback_procedure: Serve Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=0" over valid HTTPS (do not just remove the header); pinned clients recover on next visit
tradeoffs: Phase-1 seven-day pin: weaker protection than a one-year pin, but recovery window during the static->WordPress migration is days, not a year
Phase 2 one-year pin only after migration is stable
residual_risks: Certificate renewal reliability inferred (CT logs unavailable, EVID-0030); Hostinger-managed Let's Encrypt
human_decisions_required: Momento de activación tras WordPress estable (ADR 0020)

title: CSP is only 'upgrade-insecure-requests': no script/frame/object restrictions
affected_files: .htaccess (platform may also inject the current value)
reproduction_steps: curl -sS -D - https://caminodeldharma.org/ | grep -i content-security-policy → 'upgrade-insecure-requests' only
expected_behavior: CSP restricts script/frame/object sources to the known set (self, googletagmanager, YouTube, Vimeo)
actual_behavior: Any origin could inject/execute scripts if markup injection ever occurred; no XSS containment layer
root_cause: CSP never designed; current directive appears platform-provided
user_impact: No user-visible impact today; defense-in-depth gap
business_impact: A future compromise (e.g., of a third party) has no policy containment
recommendation: Stage a real CSP: inventory sources (done in technology.md), deploy Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only with default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://www.googletagmanager.com; frame-src https://www.youtube.com https://player.vimeo.com; img-src 'self' data: https://img.youtube.com; connect-src 'self' https://*.google-analytics.com https://*.analytics.google.com; monitor, then enforce
desired_outcome: Enforced CSP covering all present third parties without console violations
acceptance_criteria: Report-only phase shows zero violations across all 13 pages and dialogs
Enforced policy keeps GA4, YouTube, Vimeo functional
No inline-script breakage (site currently uses inline JSON-LD only — data blocks, not executable, unaffected; verify no inline event handlers)
implementation_locations: .htaccess mod_headers block; verify interaction with the platform-injected 'upgrade-insecure-requests' value
prerequisites: Confirm whether hPanel injects the current CSP and whether .htaccess can override/append
resolution_steps: Add Report-Only header in .htaccess with the drafted policy
Browse all pages + exercise dialogs while watching console/reports
Fix any violation; then switch to enforcing header retaining upgrade-insecure-requests
durable_fix: Enforced CSP after clean monitoring window
proposed_changes: Header always set Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://www.googletagmanager.com; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: https://img.youtube.com; font-src 'self'; frame-src https://www.youtube.com https://player.vimeo.com; connect-src 'self' https://*.google-analytics.com https://*.analytics.google.com https://www.googletagmanager.com; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'; form-action 'self' https://wa.me; upgrade-insecure-requests"
validation_procedure: Console shows no CSP violations on all pages in report-only
After enforcement: GA4 hits arrive; embeds play
monitoring_signals: CSP violation reports/console during report-only window
rollback_procedure: Revert header to report-only or remove; static rollback via git
tradeoffs: style-src 'unsafe-inline' likely needed initially (inline styles); acceptable first stage
residual_risks: Platform header injection may duplicate/conflict — must verify during implementation

title: Third-party video embeds may set cookies; no privacy policy published (GA4 permanently discarded per ADR 0019)
reproduction_steps: Open any page with a fresh profile
document.cookie already contains _ga and _ga_B8FY69RGSS
No banner, no policy link anywhere
expected_behavior: Analytics identifiers only after informed consent (or a consent-exempt measurement setup), plus a published data-treatment policy
actual_behavior: RESOLVED IN PART 2026-07-20. GA4 was removed in v1.0.12 and permanently discarded by ADR 0019 — production verified to serve NO first-party cookies (no Set-Cookie header, no gtag in source or production). What remains: 8 YouTube and 2 Vimeo embeds, none using youtube-nocookie, which can set third-party cookies on playback; and the site still has no published privacy policy.
root_cause: Video embeds use the standard YouTube/Vimeo domains rather than the no-cookie variants; privacy policy never drafted
user_impact: Visitors tracked without notice or choice
business_impact: Regulatory exposure risk (Colombia Ley 1581/2012 habeas data; EU visitors) — legal applicability requires counsel and is not asserted by this audit
recommendation: Two remaining items, both narrower than the original finding: (1) switch the 10 video embeds to youtube-nocookie.com and Vimeo's DNT parameter; (2) publish a privacy policy — still advisable because Ley 1581/2012 covers personal-data processing in general (contact channels), not only cookies; the legal conclusion belongs to counsel. The consent-mechanism question is CLOSED: ADR 0019 rules out cookie-based analytics permanently, so no consent banner is required for analytics.
desired_outcome: No persistent identifiers before consent (or a documented consent-exempt setup) and a published policy
acceptance_criteria: Clean profile: no cookies set on page load (already true) and none from video embeds until the user plays
10 embeds served from youtube-nocookie.com / Vimeo DNT
Privacy policy published and linked in the footer
Perfil limpio: cero cookies Y cero almacenamiento en cliente (localStorage removido 2026-07-20, EVID-0051)
implementation_locations: footer partial region of each page
prerequisites: Organizational/legal decision on consent approach and policy text
resolution_steps: Publish policy page, add footer link
Verify cookie behavior on fresh profile
minimal_safe_fix: Publish policy page + switch embeds to nocookie domain
durable_fix: Consent Mode v2 (default denied) with an accessible banner, or cookieless analytics
proposed_changes: gtag('consent','default',{analytics_storage:'denied'}) before config; banner grants via gtag('consent','update',...)
validation_procedure: Fresh profile: no _ga before consent
Cookie audit repeated on 3 templates
regression_tests: GA4 data continuity check after consent granted
deployment_sequence: Policy page first, then consent layer, then embed change
monitoring_signals: GA4 traffic drop expected post-consent — baseline before/after

title: Oversized raster images: 1000px logo (46KB served / 36KB in repo) rendered at 44px; 1000px gallery thumbnails at 159px; no srcset site-wide
affected_files: img elements in index.html and gallery.js-rendered grid
reproduction_steps: Load home on 390x844 DPR2
Compare naturalWidth vs displayed width per image
expected_behavior: Images sized close to display dimensions (≤2x for DPR2) or served via srcset
actual_behavior: logo.png 1000x1000 (46,025 B served per content-length; 36,364 B in repo) at 44px (11.4x); galeria-01/02/03 1000px at 159px (~3x); zero srcset attributes site-wide Independently corroborated 2026-07-20 by Lighthouse 13.4.0 via PageSpeed Insights: 'Improve image delivery' flags an estimated 185 KiB of savings on mobile (EVID-0047). Mobile/desktop comparison (EVID-0048) isolates the responsive gap: 185 KiB of image savings on mobile versus 42 KiB on desktop (4.4x), which is the signature of missing srcset — mobile is served images sized for large viewports.
root_cause: Single-size exports; no responsive variant pipeline (scripts/optimize-images.sh exists but no size variants)
user_impact: Unnecessary bytes and decode cost, mostly on mobile; page still lightweight overall (602KB)
recommendation: Export logo at 120px (with 240px 2x variant or single 240px), generate 400px thumbnail variants for gallery images, and add srcset/sizes on home gallery imgs and in gallery.js
desired_outcome: No image delivered at >2.5x its largest displayed size at DPR2
acceptance_criteria: logo asset ≤10KB and ≤240px
home gallery thumbs served ≤500px wide via srcset
gallery.js grid images use a thumbnail variant
visual parity confirmed
minimal_safe_fix: Replace logo.png with properly sized export (biggest single win, zero markup change)
durable_fix: Thumbnail variants + srcset in markup and gallery.js
proposed_changes: logo.png → 240x240 optimized PNG or SVG (favicon.svg already exists as vector source)
validation_procedure: Re-run image audit script: all ratios ≤2.5
Visual diff of header and gallery
regression_tests: Gallery pagination still renders images correctly

title: CSS/JS cached 7 days without any versioning: deploys can serve stale styles/scripts up to a week
reproduction_steps: curl -sI asset → cache-control: public, max-age=604800; asset URLs carry no hash/version
expected_behavior: Asset updates take effect promptly for returning visitors
actual_behavior: Browsers may use cached CSS/JS for up to 7 days after a deploy
root_cause: No cache-busting mechanism in a hand-built static site
user_impact: Stale UI/JS after releases (site version is 1.0.11 — actively iterated)
recommendation: Append ?v=<VERSION> (file VERSION already exists, currently 1.0.11) to all main.css/normalize.css/*.js references, updated on release; optionally lengthen asset max-age afterwards
desired_outcome: Deploying a new version invalidates CSS/JS immediately
acceptance_criteria: All css/js references carry ?v= matching VERSION
Changing VERSION and redeploying yields new asset URLs
resolution_steps: Add ?v=1.0.11 to asset URLs across pages
Document release step: bump VERSION + update references (or add a tiny build/sed step)
validation_procedure: View source: all asset refs versioned
Deploy test: bump version, confirm fresh fetch
regression_tests: Pages render with cached and fresh profiles
residual_risks: Some CDNs ignore query strings in cache keys — verify hcdn behavior after deploy
dependencies: PERF-001 (do image markup changes first to avoid double edits)

title: Gallery content is invisible without JavaScript: client-rendered grid with no noscript fallback
reproduction_steps: Fetch /galeria HTML: #gallery-albums is empty; images exist only as JSON in #gallery-data script block
expected_behavior: Primary page content available in served HTML or an equivalent fallback
actual_behavior: Without JS, page body has heading and empty container; image data is present but only as an app-specific JSON blob
user_impact: None for mainstream browsers; degraded for text-only/agent access
business_impact: Minor; page is marked Optional in llms.txt
recommendation: Server-render the first page of each album as static markup (progressive enhancement: gallery.js replaces/paginates), or add a noscript block with representative images
desired_outcome: Fetching /galeria without JS yields at least the first 12 images per album with alt text
acceptance_criteria: curl of /galeria contains ≥1 per album inside #gallery-albums
JS experience unchanged
resolution_steps: Pre-render album page 1 markup into galeria/index.html
Adjust gallery.js to replaceChildren on navigation only
proposed_changes: Static grid markup mirroring renderGrid() output for page 1
validation_procedure: curl body contains images
Browser behavior unchanged incl. pagination
tradeoffs: Slight duplication between markup and JSON data

title: No /.well-known/security.txt although the repo maintains SECURITY.md
reproduction_steps: curl -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://caminodeldharma.org/.well-known/security.txt → 404
expected_behavior: RFC 9116 security.txt advertises the security contact
actual_behavior: 404; disclosure policy only in the non-deployed repo file
business_impact: Security researchers lack a machine-discoverable contact
recommendation: Publish /.well-known/security.txt with Contact (mailto per SECURITY.md), Expires, Preferred-Languages: es, en, Canonical
desired_outcome: Valid RFC 9116 file served over HTTPS
acceptance_criteria: 200 text/plain with Contact and future Expires
Content matches SECURITY.md contact
durable_fix: Same + yearly Expires refresh in release checklist
proposed_changes:
Contact: mailto:<address from SECURITY.md>
Expires: 2027-07-19T00:00:00.000Z
Preferred-Languages: es, en
Canonical: https://caminodeldharma.org/.well-known/security.txt
validation_procedure: curl status/content

title: Informational: www HTTP entry uses a 2-hop redirect chain; JS served as application/x-javascript; fonts README publicly reachable
reproduction_steps: curl -L http://www.caminodeldharma.org/eventos → 2 redirects to https apex
actual_behavior: (1) http://www → https://www → https://apex (each hop a safe 301 upgrade; first hop is platform edge, before .htaccess). (2) JS content-type application/x-javascript (legacy but functional). (3) assets/fonts/README.md is public (harmless font documentation)
root_cause: Platform edge behavior and hosting MIME defaults
recommendation: No action required. Optional: check hPanel for single-hop redirect option; exclude README files from deploy

title: Weak organic visibility for broad and local queries; niche queries already rank #1 (Google CO verified)
reproduction_steps: WebSearch/Google 'budismo en colombia', 'comunidad budista colombia', 'centro budista colombia meditación', 'budismo chan colombia', 'budismo tierra pura colombia', 'budismo cali meditación', 'retiro budista colombia 2026'
Inspect top 10: caminodeldharma.org absent in all
expected_behavior: A person interested in Buddhism in Colombia finds the site via thematic searches, not only by knowing the exact name
actual_behavior: CONFIRMED WITH FIRST-PARTY DATA and REFRAMED 2026-07-20 (EVID-0052). Search Console: 9 clicks / 35 impressions total, and exactly ONE query returns data — the brand name 'camino del dharma' (5 clicks, 17 impressions, avg position 3.35). Brand-only findability is therefore verified from Google's own data, not just external observation. CRITICAL CONTEXT: the GSC daily chart holds only two dates (2026-07-17/18) because the current static site was published 2026-07-18 — the original audit ran on a ONE-DAY-OLD site. Absence from competitive thematic queries is therefore expected at this age, not evidence of a structural defect. What remains a genuine finding is the underlying authority deficit (DR 0.4 on a 7.5-year-old domain, inherited from the previous WordPress stage) and the absence of local presence and topical content.
root_cause: QUANTIFIED 2026-07-20: authority is near zero — DR 0.4 / UR 4 (Ahrefs-derived), DA 6 / PA 18 (Moz-derived), cross-checked DA 2 (Semrush-derived) — on a domain that is 7 years 5 months old, i.e. an established domain that never accumulated links. Spam Score 7% is healthy, so this is link ABSENCE, not toxicity: the fix is acquisition, not disavow. Precise cause verified across three authoritative topical sources that cite the community but do not link the domain: Buddhistdoor (links only to Facebook), EcoEspiritualidad (full listing ranking #2 for the brand, zero outbound links), budismo.com directory (not listed). Compounded by: no Google Business Profile (local packs own broad/local SERPs), minimal topical content (1 blog post), partial indexation (4 of 13 URLs in site:).
user_impact: Potential practitioners cannot discover the community organically
business_impact: Organic acquisition near zero. Competitor baseline (EVID-0046) shows the gap is CLOSEABLE: no rival exceeds DA 20 (median 17, nearest rung 8) — this is a uniformly low-authority niche with no dominant player. Reaching DA 8 is a 6-point move and, critically, does not require more links: budismocolombia.org holds DA 8 with 214 external links versus 207 here, so the deficit is link quality and referring-domain diversity, which is exactly what the three targeted link requests address.
recommendation: Priorities by verified data: (1) Google Business Profile — local packs dominate every broad/local SERP tested (TASK-0014, needs confirmed address); (2) targeted link acquisition, now specific rather than generic — ask Buddhistdoor to link the domain in the articles that already describe the community, ask EcoEspiritualidad to add the website to its existing listing, and register in the budismo.com Colombia directory (TASK-0014); (3) GSC property — Google itself advertises the domain has none connected (TASK-0015); (4) editorial content for informational queries (TASK-0016). Do NOT pursue disavow work: Spam Score is healthy.
desired_outcome: Retain #1 on niche queries; enter page 1 (organic or local pack) for 'budismo cali' and at least one broad query within 3-6 months
acceptance_criteria: GSC shows impressions/clicks for non-brand queries
#1 retained for budismo chan/tierra pura colombia
Presence in local pack or page 1 for 'budismo cali'
At least 2 topical referring domains linking to caminodeldharma.org
DA reaches 8 (first milestone, parity with the nearest competitors) re-measured with the SAME tool
minimal_safe_fix: Deploy on-page changes + GSC registration + directory/backlink outreach
durable_fix: Sustained editorial content plan targeting informational queries; review quarterly with GSC data
validation_procedure: After deploy + 4-8 weeks: repeat query battery; check GSC queries report
regression_tests: Internal SEO controls remain PASS (titles unique, canonicals, sitemap)
monitoring_signals: Referring domains (re-measure DR/DA quarterly with the SAME tool for comparability)
rollback_procedure: git revert of on-page edits if any regression
tradeoffs: Results depend on external actors (directories, backlinks) and take months
residual_risks: Rankings and indexation are still settling; conclusions about the effectiveness of authority and content work require re-measuring GSC in 4-8 weeks
Authority milestones depend on third parties accepting link requests

title: Search index polluted by WordPress-era residual URLs (test page ranks in brand SERP)
reproduction_steps: DDG search site:caminodeldharma.org → positions 2,4,5 are WordPress-era URLs
curl each → 301→404 (or 200 duplicate for ?page_id=10)
expected_behavior: Index contains the 13 canonical sitemap URLs; legacy URLs redirect (301) to equivalents or return 410
actual_behavior: Brand SERP shows a page literally titled 'prueba' (test); category archives 404 via redirect chain; ?page_id=10 serves duplicate home content GSC (EVID-0052) additionally shows Google still counting https://, https://www. and http:// as separate pages; the 301s are correct, so consolidation should follow naturally given the site's age. Watch: if they remain separate after 4-8 weeks, investigate.
root_cause: Site previously ran WordPress on this domain; static replacement shipped no redirect map for WP URLs (only 3 legacy routes were mapped)
user_impact: Brand searchers land on 404s or see 'prueba' in results — credibility damage
business_impact: Lost link equity from old URLs; crawl budget wasted
recommendation: Redirect map implemented in source .htaccess during this continuation: 410 /prueba, 301 category/* → /blog, 301 ?page_id=10 → /comunidad, 301 other page_id → /. Deploy (TASK-0013), then request removal/recrawl in GSC (TASK-0015)
desired_outcome: Legacy URLs out of the index; only canonical URLs listed for site: query
acceptance_criteria: curl https://caminodeldharma.org/prueba → 410
curl -L https://caminodeldharma.org/category/noticias → 200 at /blog
curl -sSL 'https://caminodeldharma.org/?page_id=10' → 301 to /comunidad
site: query shows no /prueba//category/ URLs after recrawl window
durable_fix: Same + GSC monitoring of coverage report
validation_procedure: curl matrix post-deploy (see acceptance criteria)
regression_tests: 3 pre-existing legacy routes still 301 to same targets
monitoring_signals: GSC coverage: 404/410 count for legacy URLs trending to zero indexed
rollback_triggers: Any canonical page caught by new rules
tradeoffs: 410 for /prueba discards any link equity (acceptable: test page)
residual_risks: Deindexing takes weeks without GSC removal requests

title: Invisible to question-form and intent-form queries: ranks #1 on keywords, absent on the same question in natural language
reproduction_steps: Google (hl=es, gl=co): 'budismo chan colombia' -> site #1
Same intent as a question: 'dónde practicar budismo chan en Colombia' -> site absent from page 1
'meditación budista online en español gratis' -> absent; winners have a dedicated online-meditation page
Brand query 'camino del dharma': AI Overview cites EcoEspiritualidad/WisdomLib/StudyBuddhism/Wikipedia, not the site, despite it ranking #1
expected_behavior: The site is retrievable and citable when a person or an AI agent asks the question in natural language, not only when the exact keyword is typed
actual_behavior: Ranking on keyword form does not transfer to question form. Question-form SERPs are held by local packs (Google Business Profiles) and by competitors with intent-specific pages. Google's own AI Overview for the brand query does not cite the site.
root_cause: Content is organized as institutional prose by topic, not as answerable blocks matched to user questions; no question-form headings, no short definitional answers, no dedicated pages for specific intents (online meditation, city, beginner), and no Google Business Profile to compete in local packs
user_impact: People and AI assistants asking how/where to practice do not find the community
business_impact: The channel where discovery increasingly happens (assistants, AI overviews, natural-language search) returns competitors
recommendation: Restructure key content into answerable units: question-form H2/H3, a 2-3 sentence direct answer under each, FAQPage structured data where genuinely applicable, and dedicated pages for the strongest intents (starting with online meditation, ASO-002). Combine with GBP (TASK-0014) for local-pack presence. Coordinate with the editorial plan (TASK-0016).
desired_outcome: Site appears for question-form queries in its niche and becomes a citable source for assistant answers about Chan/Pure Land practice in Spanish
acceptance_criteria: Page 1 presence for at least 2 question-form queries in the niche
At least one page structured as question + direct answer per key intent
GSC shows impressions on question-form queries
resolution_steps: Inventory the real questions (PAA boxes, WhatsApp FAQs)
Rewrite headings into question form with direct answers
Add FAQPage only where content genuinely is Q&A
minimal_safe_fix: Question-form headings + direct-answer paragraphs on practica and linaje
durable_fix: Sustained intent-driven content architecture reviewed with GSC query data
example_fix: H2: '¿Dónde puedo practicar budismo Chan en Colombia?' followed by a 2-3 sentence direct answer
validation_procedure: Re-run the question-form query battery after 4-8 weeks
tradeoffs: Question-form headings must not degrade the community's editorial voice
residual_risks: AI-platform citation cannot be guaranteed or directly controlled

title: Weekly online meditation — the site's highest-value recurring action — is not a citable entity
reproduction_steps: grep 'lunes' -> only prose <li> on home and practica
JSON-LD inventory: only 2 one-off Event nodes on individual event pages; no Event/EventSeries/Schedule for the weekly meditation
llms.txt: no mention of the weekly meditation
Google 'meditación budista online en español gratis' -> site absent; winners have dedicated pages
expected_behavior: An agent asked 'where can I join a Buddhist meditation online in Spanish?' can find, cite and link the community's weekly session
actual_behavior: The offering exists only as a prose line ('Todos los lunes 7:30 p. m., virtual (Zoom)') with no dedicated URL, no structured data, no llms.txt entry, no sitemap entry, and no direct join link (Zoom is provided only after human WhatsApp contact)
root_cause: The recurring practice was modeled as page copy rather than as a first-class entity; only one-off events received Event schema
user_impact: The lowest-barrier entry point (free, virtual, beginner-friendly, no geographic limit) is undiscoverable through assistants and search
business_impact: Loses the community's clearest competitive differentiator against presential competitors
recommendation: Give the weekly meditation its own URL, mark it up as Event/EventSeries with eventAttendanceMode OnlineEventAttendanceMode and weekly repeatFrequency, add it to llms.txt and sitemap, and decide whether to expose a direct join link or keep the human WhatsApp gate (community decision — document whichever is chosen)
desired_outcome: The weekly session is a linkable, structured, citable entity that agents can surface
acceptance_criteria: Dedicated URL returns 200 and is in sitemap
Valid Event/EventSeries JSON-LD with online attendance mode and weekly recurrence
llms.txt lists the session
Rich Results Test passes
prerequisites: Community decision on exposing the Zoom link vs. WhatsApp gate
minimal_safe_fix: Dedicated page + Event JSON-LD + llms.txt entry, keeping the WhatsApp gate
durable_fix: Same plus a documented join flow and per-session freshness (updated dates)
validation_procedure: Validate JSON-LD; confirm URL in sitemap; confirm llms.txt mention
regression_tests: Home and practica copy still coherent
rollback_triggers: Thin-content signal on the new page
tradeoffs: Publishing a direct Zoom link trades community control for agent-accessibility — explicitly a community decision
residual_risks: Recurring-event markup needs upkeep if the schedule changes
